extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian bridged to breakaway YATES Adam on final climb descent, caught him after crash with technical descending at 50mph
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE deliberately conserved energy on final climb knowing 30-second deficit could be erased with superior descending skills
YATES Adam prioritized breakaway infiltration on GC rest day, gambling stage win against non-climbers rather than defending position
Quick-Step's 50+ wins midseason suggests roster depth allows leaders like ALAPHILIPPE to hunt stages without sprint train obligations

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: targeting descent-finish stages while wearing polka dots reveals willingness to burn matches for jersey collection over GC
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: front wheel crash on descent suggests technical limits prevent converting superior climbing into stage wins on mountain finishes
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: negative: defending KOM winner trails by 49 points, indicating prior-year mountain performance doesn't reliably predict form repetition
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: 13:50 white jersey deficit to LATOUR shows Sky uses young talent for domestique work rather than jersey hunting
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: fifth consecutive stage finishing with identical top-3 podium suggests Pyrenees stalemate before decisive stage looms
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: late-stage burst while teammates FROOME and THOMAS control suggests freedom to probe weaknesses within team hierarchy
